subject: Public Health
title: How the Plague Spread
description: A 60-second lesson on How the Plague Spread.

hook.text: The plague traveled along medieval trade routes—the very networks that connected Europe killed it.

buildup.text: Infected fleas bit rats. When rats died, fleas jumped to humans. Pneumonic plague spread person to person.

discovery.text: Port cities fell first. From Sicily, plague reached Paris in months and London within a year.

twist.text: Some towns escaped entirely by isolating early—cutting trade routes before the plague arrived.

climax.text: Milan survived by bricking up houses with infected families inside—brutal but effective quarantine.

punchline.text: Connection spread it. Isolation stopped it.

quiz.question: How did pneumonic plague differ from bubonic plague in transmission?
options: It spread person to person through the air
It spread only through rat fleas
